library_preparation_preflight.summary: No concrete extra setup is required: the 2.2.1 artifact brings its Maven SCM/API and utility dependencies transitively, and meaningful coverage can be reached through command-line construction and parser/consumer tests without Docker, extra test dependencies, environment variables, or system properties. The reported failure is API drift in existing tests, not missing setup.
risks: The library is the Git executable provider and normal end-to-end use shells out to the local `git` CLI; tests that choose to execute real Git commands should keep repositories local/temp and avoid network remotes.
If generated tests perform real commits or tags, Git user configuration may be needed inside the test repository, but this is optional test-code setup rather than prerequisite deterministic setup.
Version 2.2.1 changed APIs around tag signing and push command construction, so existing 2.1.0 tests must be updated for `SignOption` and instance methods.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8354 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.scm:maven-scm-provider-gitexe:2.2.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015
